source_pr.title: types: fix uint64 overflow bug in `ConvertJSONToFloat`
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

In the origin version `ConvertIntToUint(sc, j.GetInt64(), IntergerUnsignedUpperBound(mysql.TypeLonglong), mysql.TypeLonglong)` , when `j.GetInt64()` return neg number such as `1<<63`, then `ConvertIntToUint` may treat it as overflow, this is a bug.

### What is changed and how it works?

Replace `j.GetInt64` by `j.GetUint64`

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test

Related changes

 - Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR fixes a deterministic uint64 overflow bug in `ConvertJSONToFloat` and includes unrelated build artifacts (`my_Makefile`, `nohup.out`). The case evidence is only a `+race: failpoint-enable` line and does not establish an intermittent flaky trigger, so keep the conservative fallback classification rather than guessing a concurrency root cause.
fix_pattern: N/A (insufficient evidence; no targeted stabilization change identified).

evidence: files: go.mod, my_Makefile, nohup.out, types/convert.go, types/convert_test.go, expression/integration_test.go, types/datum_test.go
